r_relatable_problem.primary_problem: Golfers believe their slice is caused by an over-the-top swing path, coming over the top, or not swinging from the inside. They think they need to fix their swing path, change their grip, close their stance, change ball placement, flip their wrists on the downswing, use training aids, or get special offset drivers.
relatability_triggers: Do you ever hit a slice... push... or fade?

Existing belief: Golfers believe their slice is caused by an over-the-top swing path, coming over the top, or not swinging from the inside. They think they need to fix their swing path, change their grip, close their stance, change ball placement, flip their wrists on the downswing, use training aids, or get special

s_specific_solution.core_mechanism: Reverse Slice Sequence: The Reverse Slice Sequence is a 15-shot, 2-step training process. Part 1 uses the 'Auto-Square' move that naturally brings the clubface square every time you swing. Within the first 10 practice swings, you'll see an immediate change in ball flight. This move actually OVER-CORRECTS the open clubface, getting you to hit a 5 to 10 yard draw on average. You hit 10 pulls or hooks in a row. Because you're now seeing your ball fly LEFT, you no longer get the feeling that you have to swing so it doesn't go right. This begins to automatically REVERSE your muscle memory. Part 2 uses the 'Auto-Path' move, which dials in your swing path within just 5 practice swings. So you never come over-the-top again. This doesn't require any swing thoughts or overcomplicated movements and continues to rewire your muscle memory to nail the correct swing path automatically. When you do it exactly as shown, it quickly REVERSES the habit of swinging over-the-top. The hook from the first 10 shots instantly straightens out and turns into a baby draw, because now that the swing path is coming down on-plane, the clubface is making solid, square contact with the ball. It's like a 'cheat code' for your muscle memory - destroying the bad swing habits that have been ingrained over the years and instantly replacing them with brand NEW muscle memory that produces consistently straight, long, powerful drives.
mechanism_proof_points: Eric Cogorno - golf teaching professional with 20,000+ in-person lessons over 12 years
Students fly in from Korea, Spain, UK to learn this technique at $300/hour

Top instructors from Scratch Club: James Sieckmann (#1 ranked short game coach per Golf Digest), Kevin Weeks (Top 3 putting teacher), Dr. Deborah Graham (Top 5 golf psychologist, trained 400+ tour pros), Brian Mogg (Golf Digest Top 50 Teacher 10 years)
unique_differentiators: This is the complete OPPOSITE of what you've been told about how to fix your slice. Instead of trying to fix the swing path first (which everyone else teaches), this attacks the ROOT CAUSE (open clubface) FIRST using the Auto-Square move, which then makes the swing path self-correct automatically via the Auto-Path move. Nobody else is teaching this specific 15-shot sequence.

headline.vsl_adaptation: Do you ever hit a slice... push... or fade? What I'm about to share with you will fundamentally shift your understanding of what's really going on...

lead.full_text: Do you ever hit a slice... push... or fade? Tired of watching helplessly as your ball flies right? Well, golfers are using a new technique that eliminates those slices, pushes, and fades... And provides a powerful straight shot... or baby draw... that flies... An Extra 15-30 Yards... After Just ONE Session At The Range... In fact, for 99% of golfers using this, it takes no more than 15 practice balls to start seeing long drives, straight down the middle of the fairway... And they're even hitting draws on command... Over... and over... and over again... consistently. All after just 15 practice shots using the simple technique I'm about to show you right now. And if that sounds too good to be true, don't worry... Thousands of other golfers just like you thought the same thing... until they tried it for themselves.

promise_architecture.promise_ceiling: Permanently eliminate slice/fade/push, hit consistent draws on command, gain 15-30 yards distance, save handful of strokes per round, reverse 40 years of bad habits in 15 shots

promise_architecture.failure_reframe: Every fix you've tried was a 'band-aid fix' that targeted the symptom (over-the-top swing path) instead of the root cause (open clubface). It's not your fault - you were given the wrong information. These fixes rely on complex mechanical swing thoughts that are neurologically impossible to execute consistently in the fraction of a second it takes to swing. That's why any improvement didn't last - your slice came right back like it never left.

three_part_structure.what_they_think: Golfers believe their slice is caused by an over-the-top swing path, coming over the top, or not swinging from the inside. They think they need to fix their swing path, change their grip, close their stance, change ball placement, flip their wrists on the downswing, use training aids, or get special offset drivers.
three_part_structure.what_it_really_is: The root cause of every slice, fade, or push is that the clubface is OPEN when you make contact with the ball. The open clubface causes the ball to go right, and your body's natural defense mechanism creates the over-the-top swing pattern in an attempt to stop the ball from going right. So the open clubface causes BOTH problems - the ball going right AND the over-the-top swing.
three_part_structure.why_nothing_worked: Every past attempt was a 'band-aid fix' that tried to fix the swing path WITHOUT addressing the open clubface first. That's like trying to fix a tire that keeps going flat by putting duct tape over the hole instead of replacing the busted tire. These fixes also rely on complex mechanical swing thoughts that are neurologically impossible to execute consistently in the fraction of a second it takes to complete a downswing. Any initial improvement doesn't last because the root cause was never addressed - the slice comes right back.

villain_profile.villain_motivation: Traditional golf instruction has been teaching golfers to fix the wrong thing (swing path) instead of the real root cause (open clubface), leading to an endless cycle of failed fixes, complex swing thoughts, and temporary improvements that never last.
villain_profile.villain_evidence: Thousands of golfers have tried these conventional approaches - changing grip, stance, ball placement, using training aids, offset drivers - and they all report the same pattern: temporary improvement followed by the slice coming right back. Multiple video testimonials confirm this pattern.

key_metaphors: Band-aid fixes - just covering up the problem instead of fixing the real issue
Busted tire analogy - fixing a flat tire by putting duct tape over the hole instead of replacing the tire
Chinese Finger Trap - gets you locked in, yanking your club the wrong way, and then you're stuck
Chain reaction - open clubface causes ball right, body compensates with over-the-top swing, which adds sidespin making slice worse
Cheat code for muscle memory - destroys bad habits and replaces with new ones automatically
countersells: Closing your stance doesn't work because it doesn't address the open clubface
Changing ball placement doesn't work because it doesn't address the open clubface
Flipping wrists on downswing doesn't work because it's neurologically impossible to time consistently
Training aids trying to force an inside-out swing path don't work because the over-the-top move is a SYMPTOM, not the cause
Special offset drivers don't work because they compensate for rather than fix the real issue
Trying to square the clubface in the downswing doesn't work because there isn't enough time in the fraction of a second to do it consistently
Complex mechanical swing thoughts (drop into the slot, clear the hips, swing inside out, release the club) don't work because they make the swing unnatural, inconsistent, and rob power

mechanism.name: Reverse Slice Sequence
mechanism.explanation_text: The Reverse Slice Sequence is a 15-shot, 2-step training process. Part 1 uses the 'Auto-Square' move that naturally brings the clubface square every time you swing. Within the first 10 practice swings, you'll see an immediate change in ball flight. This move actually OVER-CORRECTS the open clubface, getting you to hit a 5 to 10 yard draw on average. You hit 10 pulls or hooks in a row. Because you're now seeing your ball fly LEFT, you no longer get the feeling that you have to swing so it doesn't go right. This begins to automatically REVERSE your muscle memory. Part 2 uses the 'Auto-Path' move, which dials in your swing path within just 5 practice swings. So you never come over-the-top again. This doesn't require any swing thoughts or overcomplicated movements and continues to rewire your muscle memory to nail the correct swing path automatically. When you do it exactly as shown, it quickly REVERSES the habit of swinging over-the-top. The hook from the first 10 shots instantly straightens out and turns into a baby draw, because now that the swing path is coming down on-plane, the clubface is making solid, square contact with the ball. It's like a 'cheat code' for your muscle memory - destroying the bad swing habits that have been ingrained over the years and instantly replacing them with brand NEW muscle memory that produces consistently straight, long, powerful drives.

mechanism.uniqueness_claim: This is the complete OPPOSITE of what you've been told about how to fix your slice. Instead of trying to fix the swing path first (which everyone else teaches), this attacks the ROOT CAUSE (open clubface) FIRST using the Auto-Square move, which then makes the swing path self-correct automatically via the Auto-Path move. Nobody else is teaching this specific 15-shot sequence.

e5_mechanism_type.explanation: The Reverse Slice Sequence reframes the root cause of the slice from swing path (what everyone teaches) to open clubface. The mechanism itself (squaring the clubface, fixing swing path) uses known biomechanical principles, but the SEQUENCING (fix clubface FIRST, then path self-corrects) is the reframe. It takes a known problem (slice) and identifies a different root cause than conventional wisdom, then provides a proprietary-feeling sequence to address it. The 'Auto-Square' and 'Auto-Path' naming gives it a branded feel, pushing toward E4, but the underlying mechanism is a reframe of existing knowledge rather than truly proprietary science.
e5_mechanism_type.market_sophistication_fit: Stage 3-4: Golf instruction market is highly sophisticated. Golfers have tried dozens of fixes. This reframes the problem (it's not your swing path, it's your clubface) and provides a branded, sequenced solution (Reverse Slice Sequence with Auto-Square + Auto-Path). The 15-shot specificity and the 'opposite of what you've been told' positioning are classic Stage 4 moves.

mechanism.placement_density: Named in headline. Introduced conceptually in lead. Explained in detail in mid-body. Two sub-mechanisms (Auto-Square, Auto-Path) revealed as a one-two punch. Re-explained in FAQ section. Referenced in every CTA. Name repeated throughout close.

The villain's approach causes complex swing thoughts and temporary fixes; the mechanism provides a clear mind and permanent results.

elements.content_summary: Robert Chaskes - lifelong slicer for 25 years, fixed slice in minutes, hitting draws within 4-5 swings

elements.verbatim_quote: Within 4-5 swings, I was hitting the ball... I was drawing the ball, rather than the fade/slice that I've been hitting for now 25 years.

Those are all things very easy for me to say - very difficult for me to do.

elements.content_summary: Logical chain: open clubface causes ball to go right -> body's natural defense creates over-the-top swing to compensate -> this adds sidespin making slice worse -> it's a chain reaction. Therefore, fixing the clubface FIRST breaks the chain.

proof_inventory.proof_sequence: Opens with massive social proof number (42,000 golfers) in headline. Lead establishes 99% success claim. First testimonial (Robert Chaskes) is a skeptic-converted rapid-results proof. Then three consecutive 'last resort success' testimonials (Mike Marshall, Ben Badilla, Joe Jarmy) validate the band-aid fix narrative and confirm nothing else worked. Authority proof (Eric Cogorno credentials, Forbes feature) bridges to mechanism reveal. Mid-body uses logical proof (chain reaction, busted tire analogy, pro golfer clear mind) to validate the root cause reframe. Migues Fernandez delivers emotional peak testimonial proving the mechanism works. Post-offer section floods with rapid-results testimonials (Greg Dordone, Danny Fox, Joe Jarmy again). Scratch Club section stacks world-class instructor credentials. Close uses customer count (41,921) as final social proof before CTA.

story_beats.summary: Eric Cogorno is a golf teaching professional who has given over 20,000 in-person lessons over 12 years. He's known for his unique ability to fix ANY golfer's slice quickly.
key_details: Known for fixing slices without in-person training
Students fly in from Korea, Spain, UK paying $300/hour

story_beats.summary: After 20,000 golf lessons, Eric noticed a pattern in every golfer who came to him with a slice - they were all missing ONE key component and all trying the wrong fixes
key_details: Kept hearing: 'I'm coming over the top', 'I can't swing from the inside', 'I need to fix my swing path'
Every golfer was attempting to fix their over-the-top swing or compensate for it
All previous fixes were band-aid approaches

story_beats.summary: Eric discovered that the root cause is the open clubface, NOT the swing path. The open clubface causes the over-the-top swing as a natural defense mechanism. Fixing the clubface first makes everything else self-correct.
key_details: Open clubface is the ONE root issue at the core of every slice, fade, or push
Open clubface causes ball to go right AND causes the over-the-top swing
Body's natural defense mechanism creates over-the-top pattern to compensate
Chain reaction: open face -> ball right -> body overcompensates -> worse slice

story_beats.summary: Eric developed the Reverse Slice Sequence - a specific 15-shot, 2-step technique (Auto-Square + Auto-Path) that attacks the root cause first and makes the fix automatic
key_details: Auto-Square move naturally squares clubface within first 10 swings
Auto-Path move dials in swing path within 5 swings
Together they reverse up to 40 years of bad habits
No swing thoughts needed - works like a 'cheat code' for muscle memory

story_beats.summary: Over 42,000 golfers have used this technique to eliminate their slice and start hitting draws, many within just 15 shots
key_details: Golfers going from lifelong slicers to hitting draws in minutes
Featured in Forbes for this discovery

story_architecture.lesson_or_moral: The slice isn't caused by what you think it is. Every golfer has been trying to fix the symptom (swing path) instead of the root cause (open clubface). Once you fix the clubface FIRST, the swing path self-corrects automatically. All the complex swing thoughts and band-aid fixes were making things worse, not better.
story_architecture.persuasion_function: Build authority (20,000 lessons, Forbes feature), prove mechanism (pattern discovery across thousands of students validates the root cause theory), create identification (testimonial characters mirror prospect's experience), demonstrate transformation (multiple before/after testimonials showing rapid results)
story_architecture.story_callbacks: The pattern discovery (20,000 lessons revealing one missing component) is established in early body and then the specific root cause revelation unfolds through the middle. Testimonial characters serve as mini-stories that callback to the band-aid fix narrative. The 15-shot promise is repeated from headline through every section to close. The 'opposite of what you've been told' framing is established early and reinforced through mechanism reveal and FAQ.

I have never hit a draw with my driver... and this is the first time ever, which is why this is so exciting.'

section_sequence.key_content: Do you hit a slice? New technique eliminates it. 99% of golfers see results in 15 practice balls. First testimonial from Robert Chaskes (skeptic converted, 25-year slicer fixed in minutes).

section_sequence.purpose: Eliminate past failed solutions (band-aid fixes), position this as completely different approach
section_sequence.key_content: NOT about closing stance, changing ball placement, or flipping wrists. No training aids or offset drivers. No complex swing thoughts. The complete OPPOSITE of what you've been told.

section_sequence.purpose: Lower barrier - works for anyone regardless of age, flexibility, or injury
section_sequence.key_content: Doesn't require special flexibility, strength, athletic turns, pivots, wrist flips. Adapts to YOUR current swing. Works even if you're 'over the hill', have limited flexibility, or working around injury. Even if you've never hit a draw before. Reverses up to 40 years of bad habits.

section_sequence.purpose: Introduce Eric Cogorno's credentials and unique positioning
section_sequence.key_content: Eric Cogorno, golf teaching professional, 20,000+ in-person lessons over 12 years. Known for unique ability to fix ANY golfer's slice quickly without in-person training. Featured in Forbes. Facebook testimonials stacked.

section_sequence.purpose: Amplify emotional pain of the slice - embarrassment, frustration, ruined scores
section_sequence.key_content: Standing on tee box watching ball curve right. Hitting out of rough/woods/OB. Pit-of-stomach feeling on dogleg left. Looking at scorecard seeing thrown-away strokes. Banana balls.

section_sequence.purpose: Reveal the ONE key component causing the slice and why all past fixes failed
section_sequence.key_content: After 20,000 lessons, noticed pattern: all slicers missing ONE key component. Everyone focused on swing path, but the ROOT CAUSE is the open clubface at impact. Band-aid fixes confirmed by Mike Marshall, Ben Badilla, Joe Jarmy testimonials. These fixes may work for days/week but slice comes back.

section_sequence.purpose: Explain the chain reaction: open clubface -> ball right -> body compensates -> over-the-top swing -> worse slice
section_sequence.key_content: Open clubface causes the over-the-top swing as body's natural defense. Chain reaction. Chinese Finger Trap metaphor. Busted tire analogy. Trying to square clubface in downswing is neurologically impossible. Complex swing thoughts slow club speed and rob power.

section_sequence.purpose: Introduce the named mechanism and its 2-step process
section_sequence.key_content: Reverse Slice Sequence named. Part 1: Auto-Square move squares clubface in 10 shots, over-corrects to draw. Part 2: Auto-Path move dials in swing path in 5 shots. One-two punch. Cheat code for muscle memory. Results in baby draw within 15 shots. Migues Fernandez testimonial (first draw ever).

section_sequence.purpose: Transition from mechanism to product offer with value-building
section_sequence.key_content: Previously only available via $300/hour in-person lessons. HD video training with professional film crew, three $45,000 cameras, multiple angles, slow motion. Instant online access. Lifetime access. Like having Eric by your side. Greg Dordone, Danny Fox, Joe Jarmy testimonials.

section_sequence.purpose: Add massive value through bonus (Scratch Club 14-day free pass) with world-class instructor credentials
section_sequence.key_content: FREE 14-day pass to Scratch Club ($97 value). All-Access Pass to top golf instructors: James Sieckmann, Kevin Weeks, Dr. Deborah Graham, Brian Mogg, Eric Cogorno. Covers all areas: fat shots, wedge shots, chipping, bunkers, putting yips, 3-putts. 5-stroke reduction in 30 days. 'MasterClass of Golf.' David Fleck, Scott Barr, Nick Murtha, Jim LaCamp testimonials.

section_sequence.purpose: Anchor high price, reveal discounted price, justify value
section_sequence.key_content: Students pay $300/hour + travel ($650+ minimum) for in-person. Website lists $97. Today just $47. Plus free bonus: Behind-The-Scenes Lessons ($300 value) - 2 filmed private sessions.

section_sequence.purpose: Eliminate all risk with extreme guarantee
section_sequence.key_content: 365-Day Fix Your Slice Or It's Free DOUBLE Your Money Back Guarantee. Full year to decide. If not happy, get DOUBLE money back. 41,921 happy customers. Proven to work for 99%.

section_sequence.purpose: Classic three-option close: do nothing, DIY, or let me help you with future pacing
section_sequence.key_content: Option 1: Do Nothing (regret at next round). Option 2: Figure It Out Yourself (painful trial and error). Option 3: Let Me Help You (join 42,000+ golfers, try risk-free). Future pacing: wave of relaxation and confidence, longest truest drive of your life, golf buddies look on with respect, start winning bets.

section_sequence.purpose: Handle remaining objections through Q&A format, re-explain mechanism
section_sequence.key_content: 4 FAQs: Does it really work in 15 shots? (re-explains Auto-Square + Auto-Path), What do I get? (product details), What if it doesn't work? (guarantee restatement, 41,921 customers), How do I get started? (simple checkout process). Re-explains mechanism in FAQ as second pass.

section_sequence.purpose: Final push with urgency and emotional appeal
section_sequence.key_content: Your Slice Isn't Going to Fix Itself. No need to struggle for rest of your life. No need to waste years of trial-and-error. I've done the hard work for you. Limited time pricing. Act now.

'This is NOT the typical advice you've heard before... Because we're NOT talking about:' - countersell transition
'In case you don't know me...' - natural transition to authority building
'Well, after giving those 20,000 golf lessons over the past 12 years, I started noticing a pattern...' - discovery transition
'But There's a HUGE Problem' - pattern interrupt driving to root cause reveal
'So to get you there RIGHT NOW... Let me tell you the TRUTH about why you slice the ball...' - truth-reveal transition
'There's a better way... It's Called the Reverse Slice Sequence...' - solution naming transition
'Now I could keep talking your ear off... But I know you won't believe me until you see it all for yourself.' - transition to offer
'Are You Ready to Say Goodbye to Your Slice Forever?' - commitment question before offer
'Now, I could just give you this Reverse Slice Sequence and send you on your way... But as a new student of mine...' - bonus introduction transition
'Now you might be wondering: What If This Doesn't Work For Me?' - objection anticipation transition
'Right now, you have a decision to make...' - three-option close transition
narrative_flow.pacing_notes: Opens fast with headline promise and quick social proof. Slows into problem agitation and emotional pain. Builds through authority and pattern discovery. Peaks at root cause reveal with multiple testimonials validating the reframe. Slows again for detailed mechanism explanation. Rises for the Reverse Slice Sequence reveal and Migues Fernandez emotional peak. Drops into offer logistics. Rises with Scratch Club value stack and instructor credentials. Drops for price reveal. Final escalation through guarantee, three-option close with future pacing, and FAQ re-explanation before urgency close.
repetition_patterns: 15 shots / 15 practice balls / 15-shot sequence (repeated 15+ times throughout)
Reverse Slice Sequence (repeated 20+ times, italicized and bolded consistently)
slice, push, or fade (the three problem types grouped together, repeated 10+ times)
band-aid fix/fixes (repeated 4 times to label past failures)
open clubface (repeated 6+ times as the root cause)
Auto-Square / Auto-Path (repeated 4+ times each in mechanism sections)
draws/baby draws (the desired outcome, repeated 15+ times)
Cheat code for muscle memory (repeated 2 times)
callbacks: The 15-shot promise from the headline is reinforced in lead, mechanism reveal, offer section, FAQ, and every CTA
The 'band-aid fix' label established in early body is called back during mechanism explanation and FAQ
The open clubface root cause is established in mid-body and re-explained in detail in the FAQ section
The Auto-Square and Auto-Path moves are introduced in mechanism section and re-explained step-by-step in FAQ
The 42,000/41,921 customer count appears in headline, late body, and close section
Joe Jarmy appears twice - first as band-aid fix victim (early body), then as RSS success story (late body)
The 'cheat code for muscle memory' metaphor appears in mechanism reveal and is echoed in FAQ re-explanation

offer.unique_mechanism_claim: The only technique that attacks the ROOT CAUSE of the slice (open clubface) FIRST using the proprietary Auto-Square move, which then makes the swing path self-correct automatically via the Auto-Path move. Nobody else is teaching this specific 15-shot sequence. The complete OPPOSITE of what conventional golf instruction teaches.

sequence.text: Right now, you have a decision to make that will have a permanent effect on your golf game... And When You Think About It, You Really Only Have 3 Options: Option #1: Do Nothing... Option #2: Figure It Out Yourself... Option #3: Let Me Help You...

Think about your next round of golf when you're stepping up to that first tee with everyone watching you... And you hit that same slice, push, or fade, that keeps showing up in your game over and over again. Wouldn't you regret not taking this one simple action today?

That brings us to the second option, which is to take what you discovered today and figure everything out by yourself. If you can, then great... but most of us prefer a proven method like the one I've shown you here today.

Watch the videos... head over to your local driving range... and get yourself a bucket of balls. Following the simple directions in the videos, take just 15 shots, and watch what happens. See how long it takes before that smile finally comes off your face when you see your ball flying straighter and further than you ever have before. You're now making solid contact with a square clubface... You're no longer swinging over-the-top... And you're hitting draws on a consistent basis... You're Able To Place Shots With More Accuracy, And Your Confidence Grows With Every Shot You Take. You're even able to tackle that dogleg left with ease... with a nice draw that lands on the fairway. Now, when you hit the links with your golf buddies, and you step up to the first tee box, and everyone is watching... A wave of relaxation and confidence washes over you, because you know exactly what you're doing. You wind up and hit the longest, truest drive of your life, dead center down the fairway. Your golf buddies look on with respect. And you even start winning more bets.
Let ME take all the risk for you... Do what thousands of folks have already done... get instant access to the Reverse Slice Sequence training system right now, and try it completely risk-free.

Ultimately the choice is yours, but whatever you decide, you've got to choose now. If you come back to this page later I can't guarantee the discounted $47 price... or the bonuses... or the guarantee... will still be here.
Go ahead and click the button below to get started, and I'll see you on the first lesson in just a few minutes!

And there's no reason to put yourself at risk of wasting years of your life, that you'll never get back... By spinning your wheels... making the same mistakes... and getting the same scores you've been getting... round after round.

metadata.original_source: Performance Golf / Eric Cogorno - Jeff M copywriter
metadata.raw_swipe_file: RSS - VSL Page - Jeff M - 11_29_2020 - v2.md
metadata.central_concept: Your slice is NOT caused by your swing path or an over-the-top move - that's the symptom, not the disease. The root cause is an open clubface at impact. Your body's natural defense mechanism then creates the over-the-top swing to compensate, making the slice worse in a chain reaction. Every fix you've tried was a 'band-aid fix' targeting the symptom. The Reverse Slice Sequence is a specific 15-shot, 2-step technique that attacks the root cause FIRST: Step 1 (Auto-Square) squares the clubface in 10 shots, over-correcting to a draw and retraining muscle memory. Step 2 (Auto-Path) dials in the swing path in 5 shots. Together they permanently reverse up to 40 years of bad habits without complex swing thoughts, creating a natural, stress-free swing that produces consistent draws. Proven across 42,000+ golfers.
metadata.extractor_notes: Strong mechanism-driven VSL for the golf instruction niche. The root cause reframe (clubface not swing path) is the engine of the entire piece. The 'band-aid fix' villain label is effective and relatable - every golfer has experienced temporary fixes that don't last. The 15-shot specificity is brilliant - it's hyper-specific, measurable, and creates an irresistible 'I have to try this' feeling. Proof architecture is heavily testimonial-dependent with zero clinical studies, which is appropriate for the niche but limits credibility scoring. The three-option close with vivid future pacing (golf buddies watching, wave of confidence, winning bets) is textbook. The Scratch Club continuity upsell ($19/month after 14-day trial) is embedded as a bonus rather than positioned as a separate offer, which is a smart soft-sell approach. Notable absence: no personal struggle story from Eric himself - the authority is established through credentials and volume of lessons rather than a vulnerability-based story. The copy is a VSL page script (not the spoken VSL itself) with production notes included. Some testimonial quotes have timestamps indicating video testimonial editing directions.

fssit_candidates.statement: The root cause of every slice, fade, or push is that the clubface is OPEN when you make contact with the ball. The open clubface causes the ball to go right, and your body's natural defense mechanism creates the over-the-top swing pattern in an attempt to stop the ball from going right. So the open clubface causes BOTH problems - the ball going right AND the over-the-top swing.
